title: Mega Absol Battle Guide
summary: A fast physical [dark] attacker with [magic-bounce] — 150 Attack, 115 Speed, and [sucker-punch] priority make it a terrifying glass cannon.
strengths: 150 Attack / 115 Speed is top-tier offensive profile
weaknesses: Weak to Fighting, Bug, and Fairy
Pre-Mega turn uses pressure with no bounce

recommendedItems.reason: Mandatory Mega Stone with no alternative

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Absol is a fast glass cannon defined by [magic-bounce], 150 base Attack, and 115 base Speed. It combines high offensive pressure with a utility ability that reflects hazards and status moves — a rare combination that punishes setup leads and stall pivots simultaneously.

## Strengths
[magic-bounce] reflects stealth-rock, taunt, thunder-wave, will-o-wisp, and similar status moves. Offensively, 150 Attack and 115 Speed let Mega Absol outpace most natural Speed and threaten KOs with [knock-off] or [play-rough]. [sucker-punch] is a natural priority STAB that catches faster Scarf users when predicted right.

## Weaknesses & Answers
65/60/60 bulk is among the frailest Mega forms — essentially every super-effective hit is a KO, and even neutral priority threatens it. Fighting, Bug, and Fairy weaknesses are common in the meta. The pre-Mega turn uses pressure, so [magic-bounce] does not work until the evolution completes.

## Recommended Moveset
[play-rough] hits the Fighting and Dragon types that resist [dark] STAB. [sucker-punch] is a must for priority cleanup. [knock-off] is the main [dark] STAB and removes items. [swords-dance] is optional — a boosted Mega Absol sweeps, but its frail bulk makes setup risky.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[absolite] is mandatory. [magic-bounce] activates after Mega Evolution. Tera [dark] stacks on [sucker-punch] and [knock-off], while Tera [ghost] creates Normal/Fighting immunities and a clean Psychic immunity for defensive reroutes.

## Teammates & Synergy
Pair with fake-out and intimidate support to compensate for the bulk. Fairy and Psychic partners cover the Fighting weakness. tailwind extends the offensive window.

## Play Patterns
Switch in on a predicted status move to bounce it back. Use [knock-off] or [play-rough] to pressure switch-ins, and [sucker-punch] to finish faster threats. If the opponent plays slowly, a [swords-dance] setup can immediately end the match — but only if Mega Absol can afford the free turn.
